guide.title: How timebanking works
guide.caption: :community
guide.intro: Timebanking is a simple way for a community to share skills and help each other. You give an hour of your time to someone, and you earn an hour you can spend on help from anyone else in the community.
guide.equal_body: One hour always equals one time credit, whatever the task. An hour of gardening is worth the same as an hour of accountancy, tutoring or giving someone a lift. That is what makes timebanking fair and welcoming for everyone.
guide.step1_body: Offer a skill or lend a hand to another member — anything from teaching a language to helping in a garden.
guide.step2_body: For every hour you give, you earn one time credit, recorded in your wallet.
guide.step3_body: Use your credits to receive help from anyone in the community, whenever you need it.
guide.getting_started_body: Browse what members are offering and asking for, create your own listing to offer a skill, and connect with people near you.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.caption: :community
faq.intro: Answers to common questions about timebanking.
faq.a1: A time credit is one hour of your time. You earn a credit for every hour you give, and spend credits to receive help from others.
faq.q2: Is everyone's time worth the same?
faq.a2: Yes. One hour always equals one time credit, whatever the task. This is what makes timebanking fair.
faq.a3: Create a listing to offer a skill or ask for help, browse what others are offering, and connect with members near you.
faq.q4: How do I send credits to someone?
faq.a4: Open your wallet, search for the member, choose an amount and send. Credits move immediately.
faq.a5: You control what other members can see in your privacy settings, and you can export or delete your data at any time.

about.caption: :community
about.title: About :name
about.description: :name is a modern time banking platform where every hour of service is valued equally. We help communities exchange skills, build trust, and create a fairer local economy — one hour at a time.

how_it_works.title: How it works
how_it_works.subtitle: Getting started is simple. Four steps to join and begin exchanging skills in your community.

steps.title: Create your profile
steps.description: Sign up for free and list the skills you can offer to your community.

steps.title: Find what you need
steps.description: Browse listings to discover services offered by members near you.

steps.title: Exchange services
steps.description: Connect with members and arrange skill exchanges that work for both of you.

steps.title: Earn and spend credits
steps.description: Earn one time credit for every hour you give, and spend them on services you need.

values.title: Our values
values.subtitle: The principles that guide everything we do at :name.

items.title: Equality
items.description: Every person's time is valued equally. One hour of gardening is worth the same as one hour of tutoring.

items.title: Community
items.description: We believe in building strong local connections. Every exchange strengthens the fabric of your neighbourhood.

items.title: Trust and safety
items.description: Reviews, ratings, and broker oversight ensure a safe environment for all members to participate.

items.title: Sustainability
items.description: By sharing skills locally, we reduce waste, support circular economies, and strengthen local resilience.

credits.creator: Creator
credits.research_description: Informed by a social impact study commissioned by the West Cork Development Partnership.
credits.license_text: Licensed under the GNU Affero General Public License v3 (AGPL-3.0).

cta.title: Ready to get involved?
cta.description: Whether you want to share your skills or find help with something, our community is here for you.

trust_safety.caption: :community
trust_safety.title: Trust and safety
trust_safety.subtitle: :name connects neighbours so they can share skills using time credits. This page explains how that works in practice — what the platform does, what it does not, and how you can stay safe.
trust_safety.safeguarding_body: If you are worried about a member, an exchange, or something you have seen on the platform, please tell us. We respond to safeguarding reports within one working day.

how_exchanges.heading: How exchanges work
how_exchanges.intro: Exchanges are arranged between members. Here is the usual flow.
items: Find a member offering what you need, or post a request describing what you are looking for.
Message them through the platform to agree the details — time, place, and what is involved.
Complete the exchange. You only meet in person if you both want to.
Log the hours. Both members confirm — that is the receipt.
Time credits transfer automatically. One hour of help is one time credit, no matter what skill was shared.

what_we_do.heading: What we do
items: Verify every member's email address at registration, and offer optional photo-ID verification with a Verified Member badge.
Show reviews and ratings on member profiles so the community can see who has been a reliable exchange partner.
Ask every member to accept our community guidelines and acceptable use policy, and to keep to them.
Give every listing, message, and profile a report button that goes straight to coordinators.
Have human coordinators who can mediate, support, and step in when something goes wrong.
Connect with other timebanks across regions through federation, so reputation can travel with you.

what_we_dont.heading: What we do not do
what_we_dont.intro: It is important to be clear about the limits of the service.
items: We do not vet, train, or certify members for specific services. We are not a recruitment agency.
We do not provide insurance cover for the work members do for each other.
We do not supervise exchanges or act as anyone's employer or service provider.
We do not guarantee any specific outcome, level of skill, or punctuality. Reviews are how the community keeps itself accountable.

precautions.heading: Precautions you should take
precautions.intro: A few sensible steps keep everyone safer.
items: Get to know someone through the platform before you meet.
Meet in a public place where you can, especially the first time.
Tell someone you trust where you are going and when.
Trust your instincts — if something does not feel right, stop and tell us.

vetting.heading: Background checks and vetting
vetting.intro: Some services need vetting by law.
items: Some exchanges, for example childcare, elderly care, or support for vulnerable adults, may legally require a background check or vetting under local law.
Members offering these services are responsible for holding current, valid vetting where their jurisdiction requires it.
You are entitled to ask to see vetting before you agree to an exchange.
The platform does not hold vetting on members' behalf and does not verify it.

insurance.heading: Insurance
items: The platform does not provide insurance for exchanges.
Members are responsible for their own insurance where they need it.
You remain legally responsible for your own actions during an exchange.
Check whether your existing cover applies before taking part in higher-risk activities.

disputes.heading: Dispute resolution
disputes.intro: If something goes wrong, here is how we help.
items: Talk to the other member first — most issues are simple misunderstandings.
If you cannot resolve it, report it and a coordinator will look into it.
Coordinators can mediate, adjust logged hours, or take action on accounts.
Serious matters may be escalated outside the platform where necessary.

responsibilities.heading: Your responsibilities
responsibilities.intro: As a member of the community you agree to:
items: Be honest about what you offer and what you need.
Treat other members with respect.
Turn up when you say you will, or give as much notice as you can.
Log hours accurately and confirm exchanges promptly.
Report anything unsafe or against the guidelines.

rights.heading: Your rights
items: To be treated with respect and kept safe.
To control your personal information and privacy settings.
To report concerns and have them taken seriously.
To leave the community and delete your account at any time.

trust_safety.contact_cta_title: Have a question we have not answered?
trust_safety.contact_cta_body: Get in touch and a coordinator will get back to you. Most messages are answered within one working day.

accessibility.caption: :community
accessibility.title: Accessibility statement
accessibility.intro: This statement applies to the accessible version of :name. We want as many people as possible to be able to use this service.
accessibility.commitment_body: We believe the internet should be usable by everyone, regardless of ability or disability. This accessible service is built with semantic HTML, clear structure, standard form controls and progressive enhancement, so it works well with keyboards, screen readers and low-bandwidth connections.
accessibility.conformance_body: This service aims to conform to the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) version 2.2 Level AA. We assess against this standard and fix issues we find. Where any part is not yet fully conformant, we say so below and work to put it right.

keyboard.title: Keyboard navigation
keyboard.description: Full keyboard support, including a skip link, visible focus indicators, and a logical tab order.

visual.title: Visual accessibility
visual.description: A minimum 4.5:1 text contrast ratio, resizable text, and no information conveyed by colour alone.

screen_reader.title: Screen reader support
screen_reader.description: Semantic HTML, ARIA labels where needed, and meaningful alternative text for images.

responsive.title: Zoom and responsive layout
responsive.description: Content adapts to all screen sizes and supports up to 200% zoom without loss of functionality.

accessibility.limitations_body: We are continually improving this service. Some content provided by community members, or features that are still in beta, may not yet fully meet WCAG 2.2 AA. If you find a problem, please tell us using the details below.
accessibility.feedback_body: If you find an accessibility problem, or you need information on this service in a different format, please contact us. We aim to respond to accessibility feedback within 5 working days.
accessibility.testing_body: This service is tested with automated accessibility checks and with keyboard and screen-reader testing as part of our development process. Testing is ongoing as new pages are added.

wellbeing.warning_cancellation: Your cancellation rate is higher than usual.
wellbeing.warning_hours: Your logged hours are dropping significantly.
wellbeing.warning_engagement: It has been a while since your last activity.
wellbeing.no_warnings: No wellbeing warnings right now. Keep up the good balance.
wellbeing.checkin_hint: Record a quick mood check-in. This helps you track how volunteering is going.
wellbeing.note_hint: Anything you want to remember about today.
wellbeing.checkin_failed: Your check-in could not be saved. Please try again.

recommended.title: Recommended for you
recommended.description: Shifts suggested for you, based on your skills, location and availability.
recommended.empty: We have no shift suggestions for you right now. Add your skills to your profile to improve recommendations.

donations.title: Donations and giving
donations.caption: Volunteering
donations.description: Support your community with a money donation, and follow how active fundraising campaigns are doing.
donations.intro_desc: Donations are money gifts to support the community. They are not time credits. Time credits are still earned only by logging approved volunteer hours.
donations.giving_days_empty: There are no active fundraising campaigns right now.
donations.progress_aria: Fundraising progress: :percent percent of the goal
donations.history_empty: You have not made any donations yet.
donations.form_intro: Record a donation by bank transfer or PayPal. An administrator will confirm it once the payment has arrived.
donations.amount_hint: A whole or decimal amount, for example 25 or 25.50.
donations.campaign_hint: Choose a fundraising campaign, or leave as a general community donation.
donations.method_hint: How you will send the donation.
donations.message_hint: An optional note shown with your donation.
donations.anonymous_hint: Hide your name from public donor lists.
donations.donate_recorded: Thank you. Your donation has been recorded and is awaiting confirmation of payment.
donations.donate_failed: Your donation could not be recorded. Please try again.
donations.error_amount: Enter a donation amount greater than zero
donations.error_amount_max: Enter a donation amount within the allowed limit
donations.error_validation: Check your answers and try again

group_signups.title: Group sign-ups
group_signups.caption: Volunteering
group_signups.description: Shift reservations your group has made. Group leaders can add and remove members or cancel the reservation.
group_signups.empty: You are not part of any group shift reservations yet. A group leader can reserve a block of slots for your team.
group_signups.no_members: No members have been added yet.
group_signups.add_member_hint: Enter the member ID of the person you want to add to this reservation. You can find member IDs on a member profile page.
group_signups.cancel_warning: Cancelling releases all reserved slots and removes every member from this reservation. This cannot be undone.
group_signups.success_member_added: The member has been added to the reservation.
group_signups.success_member_removed: The member has been removed from the reservation.
group_signups.error_member_add_failed: The member could not be added. Check that you lead this group and that slots are still available.
group_signups.error_member_remove_failed: The member could not be removed. Check that you lead this group.
group_signups.error_reservation_cancel_failed: The reservation could not be cancelled. Check that you lead this group.

expenses.title: My expenses
expenses.caption: Volunteering
expenses.description: Claim back costs such as travel, meals or supplies from an organisation you volunteer for. Each claim is reviewed before it is paid.
expenses.empty: You have not submitted any expense claims yet.
expenses.form_intro: Tell us what the cost was for and how much you spent. Your claim will be sent to the organisation for review.
expenses.no_organisation: You need to be an approved volunteer with an organisation before you can claim expenses.
expenses.org_hint: The organisation you are claiming the expense from.
expenses.amount_hint: A whole or decimal amount, for example 12 or 12.50.
expenses.currency_hint: A currency code such as EUR or GBP. Leave blank to use the community currency.
expenses.description_hint: A short note explaining the cost.
expenses.success_submitted: Your expense claim has been submitted and is awaiting review.
expenses.error_amount_invalid: Enter an amount greater than zero
expenses.error_validation: Your claim could not be submitted. Check your answers and any expense limits, then try again.
expenses.error_forbidden: You are not allowed to claim expenses from this organisation.
expenses.error_not_found: That organisation could not be found.
expenses.error_failed: Your claim could not be submitted. Please try again.

safeguarding.title: Safeguarding
safeguarding.description: Log your safeguarding training and report any concerns or incidents. All information is handled confidentially by your community administrators.
safeguarding.no_training: You have not logged any training records yet.
safeguarding.report_incident_title: Report a safeguarding concern or incident
safeguarding.report_notice: Use this form to report a concern, allegation, near-miss, or any incident that may relate to the safety or welfare of a person. Your report will be reviewed confidentially by a designated person.
safeguarding.incident_description_hint: Describe what happened, when, and who was involved. Be as specific as possible. Minimum 20 characters.
safeguarding.no_incidents: You have not submitted any incident reports.
safeguarding.success_training_added: Your training record has been logged and is awaiting verification.
safeguarding.success_incident_reported: Your report has been submitted. A designated person will review it confidentially.
safeguarding.error_training_name_required: Enter the name of the course or programme
safeguarding.error_training_date_required: Enter the date you completed the training
safeguarding.error_training_failed: Your training record could not be saved. Please try again.
safeguarding.error_incident_title_required: Enter a brief title for the incident
safeguarding.error_incident_description_short: Describe what happened in at least 20 characters
safeguarding.error_incident_failed: Your report could not be submitted. Please try again.
safeguarding.error_generic: There was a problem. Please check your answers and try again.

manage.title: Manage your credits
manage.caption: Time credits
manage.description: See your pending credits at a glance, send credits to a member, or donate to the community fund.

balance.heading: Your balance
balance.label: Available balance
balance.pending_badge_in: {0} No pending credits|{1} Pending in: :count hour|[2,*] Pending in: :count hours

stats.heading: Summary
stats.pending_hint: Incoming and outgoing credits that have not yet completed.

transfer.heading: Send credits to a member
transfer.description: Search for a member by name, then choose how many hours to send.
transfer.prefill_notice: A recipient has been pre-selected from your link. Check the details before sending.
transfer.search_hint: Type a name and select Search.
transfer.amount_hint: For example, 1 or 2.5. You can send up to 1000 hours.
transfer.note_hint: The recipient will see this with the transfer.

donate.heading: Donate credits
donate.description: Give some of your time credits to the community fund, or directly to another member.
donate.credits_not_money: This donates your time credits to a shared community pool. It is not a money donation.
donate.warning: Donations move credits one way and cannot be undone.
donate.target_legend: Who would you like to donate to?
donate.target_fund_hint: A shared pool any member can draw on.
donate.target_member_hint: Search for the member below before donating.
donate.recipient_required: Search for and select a member to donate to first.
donate.message_hint: A short note to go with your donation.

states.donate_sent: Thank you. Your donation has been made.

errors.invalid: Enter a valid amount and recipient.
errors.insufficient: You do not have enough credits for that.
errors.not_found: That member could not be found.
errors.self: You cannot send credits to yourself.
errors.inactive: That member cannot receive credits right now.
errors.failed: Something went wrong. Please try again.

footer.wallet_link: View your full wallet and transaction history
